vulnerability.reason: shopper trust and inability to detect distortions covered, but retailer trust and verification lack omitted
vulnerability.reference: Consumers and retailers trust generated fashion images as accurate representations without mechanisms to verify body type fidelity.
vulnerability.generated: Shoppers' default trust in catalog authenticity and inability to detect AI-generated anatomical distortions allows biased garment representations to influence purchase decisions.

impact.reason: reference describes financial loss and brand damage to retailers; generated describes shopper dissatisfaction and exclusionary norms
impact.reference: Financial loss from returns and brand damage due to misrepresented clothing on underrepresented bodies.
impact.generated: Underrepresented body type shoppers purchase ill-fitting clothing, causing dissatisfaction, increased returns, and reinforcement of exclusionary beauty norms.

dose_response.reason: causal chain from biased generation through trust to returns and exclusionary norms is mostly present, but financial loss link is missing
dose_response.reference: Masked diffusion transformer-based pose-conditioned generation yields biased anatomically implausible images, which online shoppers trust, causing financial loss from returns and reinforcing narrow beauty standards against underrepresented bodies.
dose_response.generated: Masked diffusion transformer-based pose-conditioned generation of anatomically biased images, through online shoppers viewing AI-generated fashion models, triggers their default trust and inability to detect distortions, leading to ill-fitting clothing purchases by underrepresented body types, dissatisfaction, returns, and exclusionary norms.
